servinfo.title: Precautions for Diagnosing Trouble
servinfo.ptxt: To ensure that the trouble diagnosis is done accurately and smoothly, observe the following and follow ESP® System Check .

item.ptxt: Before using SUZUKI scan tool, read its “Operator’s (Instruction) Manual” to know how to use it.
If the vehicle was operated in any of the following conditions, ABS warning light and ESP® warning light may light momentarily but this does not indicate any fault in ESP® system.
The vehicle is driven with parking brake applied.
The vehicle is driven with brake dragging.
The vehicle is stuck in mud, sand, etc.
Wheels are rotated while the vehicle is jacked up.

Be sure to follow trouble diagnosis procedure described in ESP® System Check . If trouble diagnosis procedure is not followed properly, incorrect diagnosis may result. (If incorrect procedure is performed, other DTC may be stored in the ESP® hydraulic unit / control module assembly.)
When disconnecting ESP® control module connector (1), pull up the lock lever (2) of connector.
When connecting the connector, set the connector in position on ESP® hydraulic unit / control module assembly and pull down the lock lever (2) until it locks.
Communications between ECM, BCM, ESP® control module, DLC, steering angle sensor and combination meter are established via CAN.
Therefore, be sure to read Precautions for Installing Mobile Communication Equipment before inspection and handling CAN communication line.
